steps.title: Identify the relationship between the clauses
steps.content:
The strongest signal is that the second clause restates what the first clause means.

The first says cheeses are valued for “regional authenticity,” and the second explains this means using local ingredients and methods.

This restatement pattern requires a transition that signals clarification or elaboration.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
“That is,” means “in other words” or “to clarify”—the perfect signal for when one clause elaborates on another.

The other transitions don't fit: “still” implies something unexpected or contradictory, “rather” signals contrast between ideas, and “then” suggests sequence or consequence.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
